researchQuestions.title: Building Trust in AI Conversations
researchQuestions.question: How can I make AI responses transparent without making the conversation feel heavy?
researchQuestions.whyItMatters: People can trust AI too much or not enough. I want interfaces that show confidence clearly so users can make better decisions.
researchQuestions.currentDirection: I am testing lightweight interaction cues that reveal uncertainty and reasoning depth while keeping replies natural.

researchQuestions.title: Better Speaking Evaluation for Real Learners
researchQuestions.question: How can I score speaking quality while preserving the acoustic detail learners need for useful feedback?
researchQuestions.whyItMatters: Many assessment systems miss pauses, hesitation, and pronunciation detail that teachers and learners care about.
researchQuestions.currentDirection: I am combining ASR, multimodal LLMs, and grammar/relevance signals to produce feedback that is both accurate and teachable.

researchQuestions.title: Multimodal Scoring Beyond Transcript-Only Pipelines
researchQuestions.question: What is the best way to combine text, audio, and context in speaking evaluation systems?
researchQuestions.whyItMatters: Transcript-only pipelines miss timing and prosody, while audio-only pipelines lose semantic clarity.
researchQuestions.currentDirection: I am comparing score-fusion and representation-fusion methods to keep acoustic precision and semantic reasoning together.

publications.title: The NTNU System at the S&I Challenge 2025 SLA Open Track
publications.authors: HY Lin, TH Lo, YH Fang, JK Lin, CC Wang, HC Lu, B Chen
publications.impact: I reached near-top challenge performance by fusing wav2vec acoustic features with Phi-4 multimodal reasoning.
publications.summary: This work shows that combining complementary modalities can outperform single-path SLA systems.

publications.title: Advancing Automated Speaking Assessment Leveraging Multifaceted Relevance and Grammar Information
publications.authors: HC Lu, JK Lin, HY Lin, CC Wang, B Chen
publications.impact: I improved multi-aspect speaking assessment by adding richer relevance signals and fine-grained grammar features.
publications.summary: Ablation results show clear gains when question, image, and exemplar grounding are paired with detailed grammar signals.

publications.title: Acoustically Precise Hesitation Tagging Is Essential for End-to-End Verbatim Transcription Systems
publications.authors: JK Lin, HC Lu, CC Wang, HY Lin, B Chen
publications.impact: I measured strong relative WER gains by preserving realistic hesitation tags during fine-tuning.
publications.summary: The result shows hesitation fidelity is core model behavior, not just a formatting detail.

projects.name: d1-manager
projects.description: I built a web app and API for Cloudflare D1, including natural-language query support for faster debugging.

projects.name: smart-whisper
projects.description: I built a Node.js addon for whisper.cpp with model offloading and management so teams can ship speech features faster.

projects.name: LeetCode-Stats-Card
projects.description: I created embeddable coding-profile cards that made developer telemetry easy to reuse across GitHub and personal sites.

demos.name: vocal-separation
demos.description: I built this speech/music separation demo to show real inference behavior under noisy mixtures.

demos.name: audio-super-resolution
demos.description: I built this restoration demo to show how perceptual quality recovers from degraded audio inputs.

demos.name: MP-SENet
demos.description: I packaged my speech enhancement line into a public demo so people can test denoising behavior directly.

nextSteps.title: Co-build Trust-Aware Conversation Loops
nextSteps.description: Work with me on interaction loops that show confidence and uncertainty without breaking natural conversation.

nextSteps.title: Ship Multimodal Speaking Products
nextSteps.description: Partner with me to turn multimodal scoring research into reliable product features for learning and coaching.

nextSteps.title: Grow Open Speech Infrastructure
nextSteps.description: Collaborate with me on open-source tools that move speech and agent systems from prototypes to production.

taglines: I am a Researcher of Artificial Intelligence
I am a Researcher of Human-Computer Interaction
I am a 1st Year Graduate Student at NYCU
I am a ex-GDG on Campus Lead

promptChips.label: What are you building now?
promptChips.prompt: What are you currently building, and what problem are you solving?

promptChips.label: Tell me about your research
promptChips.prompt: What is your research focus in AI and HCI, and what are you working on?

promptChips.label: Your journey into tech
promptChips.prompt: How did you get into programming and technology? What shaped your path?

promptChips.label: How was this site built?
promptChips.prompt: What technologies and ideas went into building this personal site?

research-hci.label: Deep dive: HCI trust calibration
research-hci.prompt: Deep dive into your HCI direction: how are you designing conversational systems that improve trust calibration and transparency without overwhelming users?

research-speech.label: Deep dive: Speech science
research-speech.prompt: Explain your speech science focus: what specific problems in spoken language assessment and verbatim transcription are you currently solving?

research-multimodality.label: Deep dive: Multimodality
research-multimodality.prompt: Walk me through your multimodal approach: how do text, audio, and visual cues get fused in your current research systems?

paper-si-2025.label: Ask about S&I Challenge 2025
paper-si-2025.prompt: Tell me the full story behind your S&I Challenge 2025 system: model stack, score fusion strategy, and what made it rank near the top.

paper-asa-relevance.label: Ask about multifaceted ASA
paper-asa-relevance.prompt: Explain your multifaceted relevance and grammar approach for automated speaking assessment, including the key ablation findings.

paper-hesitation-tagging.label: Ask about hesitation tagging
paper-hesitation-tagging.prompt: Why is acoustically precise hesitation tagging essential in your verbatim transcription work, and how much gain did it deliver over plain schemes?

project-d1-manager.label: Ask about d1-manager architecture
project-d1-manager.prompt: Give me an architecture walkthrough of d1-manager and explain how you designed the AI-assisted query workflow.

project-smart-whisper.label: Ask about smart-whisper tradeoffs
project-smart-whisper.prompt: Explain the engineering tradeoffs behind smart-whisper, especially model offloading, runtime performance, and developer ergonomics.

project-leetcode-card.label: Ask about LeetCode-Stats-Card
project-leetcode-card.prompt: What made LeetCode-Stats-Card scale and stay useful over time, and what product decisions mattered most?

demo-vocal-separation.label: Ask about vocal separation demo
demo-vocal-separation.prompt: Walk me through your vocal separation demo pipeline and how it connects to your broader speech and audio research agenda.

demo-audio-super-resolution.label: Ask about audio super-resolution
demo-audio-super-resolution.prompt: Explain your audio super-resolution demo: what model choices, data assumptions, and failure cases should people understand?

demo-mpsenet.label: Ask about MP-SENet demos
demo-mpsenet.prompt: How did you package MP-SENet into usable public demos, and what did deployment teach you about real-world speech enhancement usage?

next-research-collab.label: Collaborate on research
next-research-collab.prompt: I want to discuss research collaboration with you. Can you share your current open problems, preferred collaboration format, and what partners should prepare?

next-product-collab.label: Collaborate on productization
next-product-collab.prompt: I want to explore productizing your conversational and speech research. What near-term product directions are you most interested in building?

next-oss-collab.label: Collaborate on OSS
next-oss-collab.prompt: I am interested in contributing to your open-source stack. Which repos are best entry points and what contribution areas need help most?
